# NASA

## Happy 250th Birthday America

Happy 250th to the U.S.A., from 250 miles above Earth! NASA astronauts celebrate the nation’s birthday from the International Space Station and look ahead to America’s next chapter of exploration.

- [Watch](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=75-H-i9ctkE)

### Featured Video

### What’s Up

This month, skywatchers can enjoy a predawn meetup of the Moon and planets; an excellent opportunity to view the Milky Way; and Saturn’s rings tilted at a striking new angle.

- [NASA's Skywatching Tips](https://science.nasa.gov/solar-system/whats-up-july-2026-skywatching-tips-from-nasa/)

### Good Morning, Earth!

A bright orange sunburst illuminates Earth’s atmosphere during an orbital sunrise in this photograph from the International Space Station as it orbited 264 miles above the Caucasus Mountains.

- [Browse Image Archive](https://www.nasa.gov/image-of-the-day/)

### Freedom 250

## NASA Celebrates America’s 250th Birthday

Our spirit of adventure and innovation will raise our nation to new heights.

From the earliest days of exploration, to the first steps on the Moon and the missions shaping our future, NASA represents the spirit of discovery that defines our nation. As the United States approaches its semiquincentennial, Freedom 250 highlights how innovation, courage, and scientific leadership have carried America forward — and how NASA continues to expand the frontier for the next generation.

- [about NASA Celebrates America’s 250th Birthday](https://www.nasa.gov/freedom250/)

![](https://images-assets.nasa.gov/image/NHQ202601170085/NHQ202601170085~large.jpg?w=1920&h=1366&fit=clip&crop=faces%2Cfocalpoint)

NASA’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket and Orion spacecraft, secured to the mobile launcher, are rolled off the Vehicle Assembly Building to Launch Pad 39B on Jan. 17, 2026, at Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

### NASA/Joel Kowsky
